reason: The missing `DataRecordResponse` hint in s1 has low user impact. `DataRecordResponse` is already covered by s3's hints with 7 relevant terms including `DataRecordResponse` itself — the section that contains the actual implementation details. s1 only mentions it in a summary overview list of all classes used across all download methods. A user querying about `DataRecordResponse` would be correctly routed to s3 via its hints. Adding it to s1 would cause redundant retrieval of a summary section that adds no implementation value beyond what s3 provides. The finding persisted because Phase E produced output identical to Phase D input — the fix loop did not apply the change despite identifying it.

findings_assessment.justification: `DataRecordResponse` is fully covered by s3's hints array and s3 contains the complete implementation details for that class. s1 only lists it in an introductory overview alongside all download-related classes. Retrieval for `DataRecordResponse` queries will correctly surface s3 regardless of this omission. Adding it to s1 would cause duplicate section retrieval without providing additional useful content. The fix was not applied in Phase E — the output JSON is identical to Phase D input, indicating the improvement loop silently skipped the correction.
